title: Ms. Mamta vs Executive Engineer on 26 May, 2023
body: Ms. Mamta vs Executive Engineer on 26 May, 2023 IN THE COURT OF SUMIT DASS, ADDITIONAL DISTRICT JUDGE­04, SOUTH WEST DISTRICT, DWARKA COURTS, NEW DELHI. RCA No.48 of 2023 In the matter of: 1. Ms. Mamta D/o late Sh. Leela Ram Dutta, R/o H. No.RZB­78, Gali No.9, Mahavir Enclave Part­I, New Delhi­110045. 2. Smt. Neelam W/o late Sh. Yashpal Mehta, R/o H. No.RZB­72, Gali No.9, Mahavir Enclave Part­I, New Delhi­110045. 3. Smt. Mamta Banduni W/o Sh.Mahima Banduni, R/o H. No.RZC­18, Gali No.9, Mahavir Enclave Part­I, New Delhi­110045. .......Appellants. VERSUS 1. Executive Engineer, M­37, Delhi Jal Board, Dwarka, New Delhi. 2. The Chairman, Delhi Jal Board, RCA No.48/2023 page 1 of 5 Jhandewalan, New Delhi. ......Respondents. Date of filing : 06.05.2023 Date of arguments : 26.05.2023 Date of decision : 26.05.2023 JUDGMENT (ORAL) 1. This appeal is directed against the impugned order dated 29.04.2023 passed by the Court of Sh. Ajay Kumar Malik, Ld. ASCJ­Cum­ JSCC­cum­Guardian Judge, South West, Dwarka Courts, New Delhi in Civil Suit No.536/2023 titled as " Mamta & Ors v. Executive Engineer & Ors " whereby and whereunder Ld. Trial Court had outrightly rejected the plaint U/o VII Rule 11 (d) of the Code of Civil Procedure , 1908 (in short CPC ). 2. Notice of the appeal was directed upon the opposite party. On behalf of respondent Sh. S.P. Gosain, Ld counsel had appeared accompanied with Sh. Hansraj Meena, Asstt. Engineer. TCR was also called and I have gone through the same as well. 3. For the sake of convenience the parties shall be denoted as per their nomenclature before Ld. Trial Court i.e. appellants as plaintiffs and respondents as defendants. 4. For the sake of convenience I am quoting certain part of the impugned order as hereunder: 2.The case of plaintiff is that on 22.04.2023 the labour of RCA No.48/2023 page 2 of 5 the Contractor of the defendants started digging the road/gali no. 9, Gurudwara Road, Mahavir Enclave,Part­I, New Delhi­110045 for the pipeline of drinking water which is 6 feet wide and all the plaintiffs are residing in the same gali. It is stated that the digging by the labourof the contractor of defendants, the sewage line in the gali was broken and seepage came out in the house of the plaintiff and stairs/bricks in the houses of the plaintiff were broken and there the wall of the houses of the plaintiff collapsed and cracks also came out in the houses of plaintiffs. It is stated that all the plaintiffs and residents of the same vicinity which are more than 106 in numbers have opposed the said digging work done by the contractors of defendants and requested them to do the said digging work in another gali/road but the digging work again started after some time. It is stated that plaintiffs have given a letter to Mrs. Bhawna Gaur, MLA of Palam Constituency to stop illegal digging work in gali no. 9 and have also reported the said matter to police officials but no action was taken. 3.As per Section 20A ­ Special Provisions for contract relating to infrastructure project­(1) No injunction shall be granted by a court in a suit under this Act involving a contract relating to an infrastructure project specified in the Schedule, where granting injunction would cause impediment or delay in the progress or completion of such infrastructure project. Explanation­For the purposes of this section, section 20B and clause(ha) of section 41 , the expression 'infrastructure project' means the category of projects and infrastructure Sub­Sectors specified in the Schedule. 4.In view of the above, the present plaint is rejected under Order 7 Rule 11(d) CPC .5. File after due compliance be consigned to Record Room. 5. Relevant to note herein that if I look at Section 20A of the RCA No.48/2023 page 3 of 5 Specific Relief Act it means that injunction should not be granted by a Court where granting of injunction would cause impediment or delay in the progress or completion of such an infrastructure project. 5.1 Such provisions to my mind is not made out in the instant case as there is no finding to the effect that the granting of injunction would cause impediment or delay in the progress or completion of such an infrastructure project. It is altogether a different aspect that Court could have exercised its discretion qua the said aspect at the time of disposal of interim application or at the final stage but it does not mean that the suit can be barred outrightly or in any manner hit by the provisions of Order VII Rule 11 CPC . 5.2 It is contended on behalf of respondent Delhi Jal Board that work of laying of portable water pipelines has more or less completed and the some minor damages caused have been repaired. It is also contended that colony where portable water pipelines are being laying is more or less without any sanction plan from the concerned authorities and as such it is very tedious job as DJB is laying water pipelines in such congested colony. 5.3 In any eventuality on the aspect regarding damages caused to the houses of plaintiffs they can approach the Court for recovery of damages or otherwise can move appropriate application for amendment before the Ld. Trial Court. 6. On the touchstone of Order VII Rule 11 CPC on a stand alone basis - keeping in view the plaint averments as true and correct - without going into the merits of the case at this juncture in my opinion the impugned order is unsustainable and is liable to be set aside. 7. As such the impugned order is set aside. 8. Appeal is disposed of accordingly. RCA No.48/2023 page 4 of 5 Copy of this order be sent to Ld. Trial Court along with its record. Parties are directed to appear before Ld. Trial Court on 05.06.2023. Appeal file be consigned to record room. Announced in the open Court on 26.05.2023. (Sumit Dass) Additional District Judge­04 South West District, Dwarka Courts, New Delhi. RCA No.48/2023 page 5 of 5
